forecast-period.text: A high-pressure system south of the state will move east on Wednesday allowing a strong cold front to move over South Australia later Thursday. A cooler southwest to southerly flow will gradually moderate as a stronger high-pressure system moves in from the west from Friday to be south of the state on Sunday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
Easterly about 10 knots.
Below 0.5 metres.
Southwesterly 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy.
East to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots shifting northwesterly in the late morning and afternoon. Winds reaching up to 20 knots in the west in the late evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning and afternoon.
Westerly 15 to 25 knots increasing to 25 to 30 knots in the late evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres around midday.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Westerly 25 to 30 knots turning southwesterly 20 to 30 knots during the day.
2 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 70% chance of showers.
